steps.title: Spot the opposition signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts two judgments: radio listeners rated Kennedy poorly (only 21% rated him winner), yet TV viewers considered him ultimately victorious. The signal is opposition.

steps.title: Find the transition that signals opposition
steps.content: ”Nevertheless” directly expresses opposition—”despite what just happened, the opposite is true”—matching the shift from poor radio performance to TV victory. “In other words” suggests clarification, “Therefore” consequence, and “Likewise” similarity; none convey this opposition. That matches choice D — D is correct.
